ef1f373018bf3dc962cb61bffc13e919e91850414c69556dd4ac417","dweb:/ipfs/QmP39wRaBU8pDgZC45WgjvqCtHNF3QY269VrtWRMty6jJA"]},"contracts/interfaces/IERC1271.sol":{"keccak256":"0xb5be929866fb622dc0784388ed2807ff0e9e3bc8a2c212c62f000df58b473a8b","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://2525d01e081665f19e8f8a55735d6821e27d38b00523453a27349eccb9c433b4","dweb:/ipfs/QmdcBKgS34WbWwELG1qdbrc95fuGjeiDcFMrij9mq7rSvN"]},"contracts/interfaces/IERC165.sol":{"keccak256":"0xf1ac930ce8516a0aaf27de84a1328e78cbb90ea0baf773ca30bc0559722288ec","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://cfbdf6a8d270584cb2173a3c28ed899c38963499071b5aca581ca1351bc7b1de","dweb:/ipfs/QmcPXECisPBK1vnbP5vyDLGCxH37HHgZhzskRLUhu92FAr"]},"contracts/interfaces/IERC20.sol":{"keccak256":"0x76f82df3d1b3bb77abad87a8d6bd4c17779ef51278583251ec8a3b93d1bd5c74","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://e657a0b55648a4ee835d50e76962f924ec6e437261c4af445de9e2947835a7c6","dweb:/ipfs/QmPyDctCAfKgpCvU3Mr2NpXcy4Pn127FwkXomHDDHhb4Jg"]}},"version":1}

burn(address,address,uint256,address,uin.details: Assumes the sweeper has granted allowance for the fee tokens to the burner prior to the call.

params.deadline: Deadline for the burn operation (i.e., swap), after which it will revert
params.exactFeeTokenAmountIn: The number of fee tokens collected
params.feeToken: The token collected from the pool
params.minTargetTokenAmountOut: The minimum amount out for the swap
params.pool: The pool the fees came from (only used for documentation in the event)
params.recipient: The recipient of the swap proceeds
params.targetToken: The desired target token (token out of the swap)

cancelOrder(address,address).details: Canceling an order prevents it from being retried.

params.receiver: The address to receive the tokens from the unfilled order
params.tokenIn: The token used to identify the order

emergencyCancelOrder(address,address).details: Canceling an order prevents it from being retried.

params.receiver: The address to receive the from the unfilled order
params.tokenIn: The token used to identify the order
The token used to identify the order

returns.orderData: The order data for the given token

params.tokenIn: The token used to identify the order

returns.orderStatus: The status of the order for the given token

getTradeableOrder(address,address,bytes3.details: **MUST** revert if the order conditions are not met.

params.ctx: The context of the order (bytes32(0) if Merkle tree is used, otherwise the H(params))
params.offchainInput: Off-chain input (similar to Balancer `userData`); currently unused
params.owner: The owner of the order (usually a contract)
params.staticInput: Conditional order type-specific data known at time of creation for all discrete orders

returns._0: order Tradeable order for submission to the CoW Protocol API

isValidSignature(bytes32,bytes).details: Should return whether the signature provided is valid for the provided data

params.hash: Hash of the data to be signed
params.signature: Signature byte array associated with _data MUST return the bytes4 magic value 0x1626ba7e when function passes. MUST NOT modify state (using STATICCALL for solc < 0.5, view modifier for solc > 0.5) MUST allow external calls

params.deadline: The deadline for the order to be filled.
params.minAmountOut: The minimum number of target tokens to receive (tokenOut)
params.tokenIn: The token used to identify the order

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

verify(address,address,bytes32,bytes32,b.details: Used in combination with `isValidSafeSignature` to verify that the order is signed by the Safe. **MUST** revert if any order conditions are not met. The `order` parameter is ignored / not validated by the `ICowConditionalOrderGenerator` implementation. This parameter is included to allow more granular control over the order verification logic, and to allow a watchtower / user to propose a discrete order without it being generated by on-chain logic. See https://docs.cow.fi/cow-protocol/reference/contracts/periphery/composable-cow.

params.offchainInput: Type-specific data NOT known at time of creation for a specific discrete order (or zero)
params.order: The proposed discrete order's `GPv2Order` data struct
params.owner: The owner of the order (usually a contract)
params.staticInput: Conditional order type-specific data known at time of creation for all discrete orders

burn(address,address,uint256,address,uin.notice: Swap an exact amount of `feeToken` for the `targetToken`, and send proceeds to the `recipient`.

cancelOrder(address,address).notice: Return tokens from an order that has failed.

emergencyCancelOrder(address,address).notice: Emergency return tokens from an order regardless of status.

getOrder(address).notice: Get the order at the sell token.

getOrderStatus(address).notice: Get the status of the order at the sell token.

getTradeableOrder(address,address,bytes3.notice: Get a tradeable order that can be posted to the CoW Protocol API and would pass signature validation.

retryOrder(address,uint256,uint256).notice: Retry an order that has not been filled yet and expired.

verify(address,address,bytes32,bytes32,b.notice: Verify that a given discrete order is valid.
